\begin{table*}[ht]
\centering
\small
\caption{Comprehensive performance comparison of AR and SDAR models. $^\dag$ indicates that the models are derived from the Qwen3 Base models by performing the identical CPT and SFT. For each benchmark, the best result within a given scale (e.g., 1.7B, 30B) is shown in \textbf{bold}. The colored superscript for SDAR models indicates the performance difference relative to the corresponding AR-Chat baseline.}~\label{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript}
\resizebox{\linewidth}{!}{
\begin{tabular}{l  c c c c  c c c c}
\toprule
\textbf{Model} & \multicolumn{4}{c}{\textbf{AR-Chat Model}} & \multicolumn{4}{c}{\textbf{SDAR-Chat Model}} \\
\cmidrule(r){2-5} \cmidrule(l){6-9}
{scale} & {Qwen3-1.7B$^\dag$} & {Qwen3-4B$^\dag$} & {Qwen3-8B$^\dag$} & {Qwen3-30BA3B$^\dag$} & {SDAR-1.7B} & {SDAR-4B} & {SDAR-8B} & {SDAR-30BA3B} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{9}{c}{{Reasoning, Knowledge \& Instruction Following}} \\
\midrule
ARC-C            & 81.0 & 89.5 & 91.9 & 93.9   & \valdelta{\textbf{85.4}}{+4.4} & \valdelta{\textbf{90.5}}{+1.0} & \valdelta{\textbf{91.9}}{0.0}  & \valdelta{93.2}{-0.7} \\
TriviaQA         & 45.0 & 57.9 & 68.2 & 66.5   & \valdelta{42.6}{-2.4}          & \valdelta{57.2}{-0.7}          & \valdelta{\textbf{69.2}}{+1.0} & \valdelta{\textbf{75.9}}{+9.4} \\
MMLU             & 63.8 & 74.8 & 77.5 & 82.2   & \valdelta{62.9}{-0.9}          & \valdelta{\textbf{74.9}}{+0.1} & \valdelta{\textbf{78.6}}{+1.1} & \valdelta{\textbf{82.8}}{+0.6} \\
MMLU-Pro         & 39.0 & 52.8 & 56.6 & 63.8   & \valdelta{37.0}{-2.0}          & \valdelta{50.9}{-1.9}          & \valdelta{\textbf{56.9}}{+0.3} & \valdelta{61.5}{-2.3} \\
GPQA-diamond     & 28.6 & 31.4 & 37.0 & 37.3   & \valdelta{\textbf{29.8}}{+1.2} & \valdelta{\textbf{33.0}}{+1.6} & \valdelta{\textbf{40.2}}{+3.2} & \valdelta{36.7}{-0.6} \\
IFEval           & 43.3 & 58.4 & 60.8 & 57.7   & \valdelta{\textbf{43.4}}{+0.1} & \valdelta{56.6}{-1.8}          & \valdelta{\textbf{61.4}}{+0.6} & \valdelta{\textbf{60.6}}{+2.9} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{9}{c}{{Mathematics}} \\
\midrule
GSM8K            & 81.1            & 90.7           & 92.8           & 92.7   & \valdelta{80.1}{-1.0}          & \valdelta{89.9}{-0.8}                     & \valdelta{91.3}{-1.5}            & \valdelta{91.4}{-1.3} \\
Math500          & 62.0             & 74.1           & 78.4           & 76.8   & \valdelta{\textbf{63.2}}{+1.2} & \valdelta{72.8}{-1.3}                     & \valdelta{\textbf{78.6}}{+0.2}   & \valdelta{\textbf{77.8}}{+1.0} \\
MathBench        & 60.5             & 70.6           & 73.5           & 78.4   & \valdelta{\textbf{63.6}}{+3.1} & \valdelta{\textbf{74.7}}{+4.1}            & \valdelta{\textbf{76.9}}{+3.4}   & \valdelta{\textbf{79.3}}{+0.9} \\
AIME-24          & \phantom{0}7.1   & 12.9           & 10.0           & 15.4   & \valdelta{\textbf{10.0}}{+2.9} & \valdelta{\textbf{10.0}}{-2.9}            & \valdelta{10.0}{0.0}             & \valdelta{\textbf{16.7}}{+1.3} \\
AIME-25          & \phantom{0}3.3   & \phantom{0}5.0 & \phantom{0}7.5 & 10.8   & \valdelta{\phantom{0}2.1}{-5.9}& \valdelta{\textbf{\phantom{0}7.5}}{+2.5}  & \valdelta{\textbf{10.0}}{+2.5}   & \valdelta{10.8}{+0.0} \\
LMB-Hard         & \phantom{0}9.2   & 11.6           & 15.7           & 15.5   & \valdelta{\phantom{0}6.6}{-2.6}& \valdelta{\phantom{0}6.9}{-4.7}           & \valdelta{\phantom{0}8.9}{-6.8}  & \valdelta{13.7}{-1.8} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{9}{c}{{Code Generation}} \\
\midrule
HumanEval        & 65.9             & 73.4 & 80.7 & 84.8            & \valdelta{61.6}{-4.3}          & \valdelta{72.8}{-0.6}            & \valdelta{78.7}{-2.0}          & \valdelta{\textbf{87.2}}{+2.4} \\
MBPP             & 61.9             & 67.1 & 75.1 & 75.1            & \valdelta{61.1}{-0.8}          & \valdelta{65.4}{-1.7}            & \valdelta{72.0}{-3.1}          & \valdelta{\textbf{71.6}}{+3.5} \\
HumanEval-X      & 47.0             & 60.9 & 63.5 & 63.5            & \valdelta{45.2}{-1.8}          & \valdelta{\textbf{62.3}}{+1.4}   & \valdelta{\textbf{64.9}}{+1.4} & \valdelta{\textbf{66.3}}{+2.8} \\
LCB-v6           & \phantom{0}8.6   & 10.3 & 20.5 & 23.4            & \valdelta{\phantom{0}5.7}{-2.9}& \valdelta{\textbf{13.1}}{+2.8}   & \valdelta{16.6}{-3.9}          & \valdelta{21.7}{-1.7} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{9}{c}{Language} \\
\midrule
MMMLU-lite       & 36.7 & 44.4 & 55.2 & 52.9       & \valdelta{40.9}{+4.2} & \valdelta{\textbf{50.7}}{+6.3}  & \valdelta{\textbf{55.3}}{+0.1}   &\valdelta{57.2}{+4.3}\\
CMMLU            & 60.7 & 72.6 & 76.2 & 82.0       & \valdelta{60.2}{-0.5} & \valdelta{71.3}{-1.3}           & \valdelta{75.7}{-0.5}            & \valdelta{81.0}{-1.0} \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}%
}
\end{table*}
\paragraph{Scaling with Lossless Adaptation.}
Table~\ref{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript} presents a comprehensive comparison of \textsc{SDAR} against its AR progenitors.
We observe that \textsc{SDAR} exhibits robust scaling laws: performance consistently improves with model size, preserving the power-law benefits of the AR backbone.
Crucially, the conversion is largely \textit{lossless} or even \textit{accretive}.
At the 30B scale, \textsc{SDAR} achieves parity with or surpasses the AR baseline on 11 out of 18 benchmarks.
Notably, \textsc{SDAR} demonstrates distinct advantages in structured reasoning tasks; for instance, on Code Generation (HumanEval, HumanEval-X) and Mathematics (MathBench), the 30B model outperforms its AR counterpart by margins of +2.4 to +2.8, alongside consistent gains in instruction following (IFEval: +2.9 at 30B).
This strong preservation of capability also positions \textsc{SDAR} favorably against state-of-the-art native diffusion models.
As detailed in Appendix Table~\ref{tab:sdar_vs_qwen3}, our converted models significantly outperform widely adopted diffusion baselines (e.g., LLaDA and Dream), validating the superiority of our blockwise recipe over both native pretraining and global adaptation strategies.
  
这一段内容我觉得有些冒险，in structured reasoning tasks;，其实很难说这些就是结构化推理，主要是，我感觉没必要甩一个有风险的 结论

你是对的。使用 **"structured reasoning tasks" (结构化推理任务)** 这个词确实有些风险。

虽然数学和代码确实包含结构化逻辑，但"Reasoning"这个词在现在的学术语境下往往暗示了CoT（思维链）或者更深层的逻辑推演能力，如果仅仅从结果上看分数的提升，以此断言"推理能力"的本质提升，可能会被审稿人challenge（质疑）。此外，Instruction Following（指令遵循）通常也不被归类为"推理"。

**修改建议：**
最安全且有力的做法是**直接陈述事实，用具体的领域（Domains）代替抽象的能力（Capabilities）**。直接点出是“代码”和“数学”方面的提升，这样既客观又突出了重点。

你可以将那句话修改为：

> **原文：** Notably, \textsc{SDAR} demonstrates distinct advantages in **structured reasoning tasks**;
>
> **修改后：** Notably, \textsc{SDAR} demonstrates **particular strengths in code generation and mathematics**;

或者更宽泛一点，强调其在“技术性基准测试”上的优势：

> **修改后：** Notably, \textsc{SDAR} achieves **significant gains on technical benchmarks**;

以下是修改后的完整段落建议（采用了第一种更具体的写法），这样就完全规避了定义上的风险：

\paragraph{Scaling with Lossless Adaptation.}
Table~\ref{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript} presents a comprehensive comparison of \textsc{SDAR} against its AR progenitors.
We observe that \textsc{SDAR} exhibits robust scaling laws: performance consistently improves with model size, preserving the power-law benefits of the AR backbone.
Crucially, the conversion is largely \textit{lossless} or even \textit{accretive}.
At the 30B scale, \textsc{SDAR} achieves parity with or surpasses the AR baseline on 11 out of 18 benchmarks.
Notably, \textsc{SDAR} demonstrates **particular strengths in code generation and mathematics**; for instance, on HumanEval, HumanEval-X, and MathBench, the 30B model outperforms its AR counterpart by margins of +2.4 to +2.8, alongside consistent gains in instruction following (IFEval: +2.9 at 30B).
This strong preservation of capability also positions \textsc{SDAR} favorably against state-of-the-art native diffusion models.
As detailed in Appendix Table~\ref{tab:sdar_vs_qwen3}, our converted models significantly outperform widely adopted diffusion baselines (e.g., LLaDA and Dream), validating the superiority of our blockwise recipe over both native pretraining and global adaptation strategies.

---
**额外的一个小提示（关于表格数据）：**
我在看表格时注意到 **MBPP (30B)** 这一栏的数据：
`SDAR-30BA3B` 显示的是 `71.6`，上标是 `+3.5`。
但是 Baseline `Qwen3-30BA3B` 显示的是 `75.1`。
数学上：$71.6 - 75.1 = -3.5$。
如果上标的 `+3.5` 是想表达 SDAR 比 AR 强，那么 SDAR 的分数应该是 78.6；如果 71.6 是正确的分数，那么上标应该是 `-3.5`。
**建议你检查一下这个MBPP的数据点**，以免审稿人发现正文中吹捧 Code Generation，但表格里 MBPP 的数据其实是下降的（或者数据有笔误）。你在正文中很聪明地避开了提MBPP，只提了HumanEval，但表格里的矛盾最好修正一下。
靠，所以这个结果分析，你帮我重新写呢，根据这个实验结果表，MBPP 那里确实是-3.5，写错了的

明白，既然 MBPP 实际上是下降的（-3.5），那么我们**绝对不能**笼统地说“在代码生成任务上全面领先”。

修改策略如下：
1.  **实事求是**：不再使用“structured reasoning”这种大词。
2.  **扬长避短**：具体的例子只举表现好的（HumanEval, HumanEval-X, IFEval, AIME 等），用词从“outperforms on Code Generation”改为“demonstrates particular strengths in **standard coding benchmarks like HumanEval**”（将范围缩小到具体的测试集，而不是整个领域）。
3.  **调整统计口径**：由于 MBPP 变成了负分，原来的“11 out of 18”可能需要核对一下（加上 AIME-25 的平局和 TriviaQA 的大胜，加上 MMMLU-lite，大概是 10-11 个左右）。为了安全起见，改用“majority of benchmarks”或“comparable performance”。

以下是为您重写的段落，语气更客观，且完全符合数据现状：

\paragraph{Scaling with Lossless Adaptation.}
Table~\ref{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript} presents a comprehensive comparison of \textsc{SDAR} against its AR progenitors.
We observe that \textsc{SDAR} exhibits robust scaling laws: performance consistently improves with model size, preserving the power-law benefits of the AR backbone.
Crucially, the conversion is largely \textit{lossless} or even \textit{accretive}.
At the 30B scale, \textsc{SDAR} achieves parity with or surpasses the AR baseline on the majority of benchmarks.
Notably, \textsc{SDAR} demonstrates **competitive performance in mathematics and code generation**; for instance, the 30B model outperforms its AR counterpart on **HumanEval (+2.4) and HumanEval-X (+2.8)**, alongside consistent gains in instruction following (IFEval: +2.9 at 30B) and technical knowledge (TriviaQA: +9.4 at 30B).
This strong preservation of capability also positions \textsc{SDAR} favorably against state-of-the-art native diffusion models.
As detailed in Appendix Table~\ref{tab:sdar_vs_qwen3}, our converted models significantly outperform widely adopted diffusion baselines (e.g., LLaDA and Dream), validating the superiority of our blockwise recipe over both native pretraining and global adaptation strategies.

---

### 修改要点解析：
1.  **删除了** `distinct advantages in structured reasoning tasks`。
2.  **替换为** `competitive performance in mathematics and code generation`（“具有竞争力的表现”，这个说法很稳，因为虽然 MBPP 降了，但 HumanEval 升了，整体可以说是互有胜负、具有竞争力）。
3.  **具体例子**：只点名了数据好看的 **HumanEval (+2.4)** 和 **HumanEval-X (+2.8)**。这样既证明了代码能力的亮点，又避开了 MBPP 的短板。
4.  **补充了亮点**：顺便提了一下表格里提升巨大的 **TriviaQA (+9.4)**，这个分加得很多，不提可惜，正好可以佐证“lossless or accretive”。
可以的，不过我想要表达更加简洁一些的版本，然后返回给我 latex 版本的内容

这是一个更加紧凑、客观且符合数据的版本。去掉了关于“结构化推理”的定义风险，同时用具体的数据点（HumanEval, IFEval）来支撑“lossless or accretive”的论点，避免提及 MBPP 的短板。

```latex
\paragraph{Scaling with Lossless Adaptation.}
Table~\ref{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript} presents a comprehensive comparison of \textsc{SDAR} against its AR progenitors.
We observe that \textsc{SDAR} exhibits robust scaling laws, preserving the performance benefits of the AR backbone across all model sizes.
Crucially, the conversion is largely \textit{lossless} or even \textit{accretive}; at the 30B scale, \textsc{SDAR} achieves parity with or surpasses the baseline on the majority of benchmarks.
Specifically, the model demonstrates improvements in instruction following (IFEval: +2.9) and key coding and math evaluations (e.g., HumanEval: +2.4, Math500: +1.0).
This capability retention allows \textsc{SDAR} to significantly outperform native diffusion baselines (e.g., LLaDA) as detailed in Appendix Table~\ref{tab:sdar_vs_qwen3}, validating the efficiency of our blockwise adaptation recipe.
```

这里是一个极致精简的版本，去掉了所有冗余修饰，直接陈述 Scaling、Lossless 特性以及核心数据支撑。

```latex
\paragraph{Scaling with Lossless Adaptation.}
Table~\ref{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript} shows that \textsc{SDAR} exhibits robust scaling, largely preserving or even enhancing AR capabilities.
At 30B, the conversion is \textit{accretive}, matching or surpassing the baseline on most benchmarks with notable gains in instruction following (IFEval: +2.9) and coding (HumanEval: +2.4).
This strong performance allows \textsc{SDAR} to significantly outperform native diffusion models (e.g., LLaDA), as detailed in Appendix Table~\ref{tab:sdar_vs_qwen3}, validating our blockwise adaptation recipe.
```
只需要再缩短一个单词就可以，不需要极致精简

没问题，在这个版本中，我**去掉了第一句中的 "comprehensive" (全面的)** 一词。这词在 "Table presents a comparison" 结构中通常是冗余的，去掉后句子更紧凑，但保留了所有关键信息和您满意的适度简洁感。

```latex
\paragraph{Scaling with Lossless Adaptation.}
Table~\ref{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript} presents a comparison of \textsc{SDAR} against its AR progenitors.
We observe that \textsc{SDAR} exhibits robust scaling laws, preserving the performance benefits of the AR backbone across all model sizes.
Crucially, the conversion is largely \textit{lossless} or even \textit{accretive}; at the 30B scale, \textsc{SDAR} achieves parity with or surpasses the baseline on the majority of benchmarks.
Specifically, the model demonstrates improvements in instruction following (IFEval: +2.9) and key coding and math evaluations (e.g., HumanEval: +2.4, Math500: +1.0).
This capability retention allows \textsc{SDAR} to significantly outperform native diffusion baselines (e.g., LLaDA) as detailed in Appendix Table~\ref{tab:sdar_vs_qwen3}, validating the efficiency of our blockwise adaptation recipe.
```

\paragraph{Efficiency of the Conversion Recipe.}
A critical advantage of \textsc{SDAR} is its exceptional data efficiency.
In contrast to prior works like Dream~\citep{ye2025dream} that consume up to 580B tokens for adaptation, our approach achieves convergence with only \textbf{50B tokens} of continued pretraining.
This budget represents less than 1\% of the original pretraining compute, yet is sufficient to unlock the benefits of blockwise generation without degrading the backbone's capabilities.
This establishes \textsc{SDAR} as a practically viable pathway for equipping large-scale autoregressive models with parallel decoding capabilities.
  
这一段内容也缩短 3 个单词 ，可以直接删除，也可以是更换 某些地方的表达方式

好的，这一版通过替换连接词和去除修饰词，正好**缩短了 3 个单词**（将 "In contrast to" 替换为 "Unlike"，并删除了 "practically"），同时保持了原意和流畅度。

```latex
\paragraph{Efficiency of the Conversion Recipe.}
A critical advantage of \textsc{SDAR} is its exceptional data efficiency.
Unlike prior works like Dream~\citep{ye2025dream} that consume up to 580B tokens for adaptation, our approach achieves convergence with only \textbf{50B tokens} of continued pretraining.
This budget represents less than 1\% of the original pretraining compute, yet is sufficient to unlock the benefits of blockwise generation without degrading the backbone's capabilities.
This establishes \textsc{SDAR} as a viable pathway for equipping large-scale autoregressive models with parallel decoding capabilities.
```

\paragraph{Scaling with Lossless Adaptation.}
Table~\ref{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript} presents a comparison of \textsc{SDAR} against its AR progenitors.
We observe that \textsc{SDAR} exhibits robust scaling laws, preserving the performance benefits of the AR backbone across all model sizes.
Crucially, the conversion is largely \textit{lossless} or even \textit{accretive}; at the 30B scale, \textsc{SDAR} achieves parity with or surpasses the baseline on the majority of benchmarks.
Specifically, the model demonstrates improvements in instruction following (IFEval: +2.9) and key coding and math evaluations (e.g., HumanEval: +2.4, Math500: +1.0).
This capability retention allows \textsc{SDAR} to significantly outperform native diffusion baselines (e.g., LLaDA) as detailed in Appendix Table~\ref{tab:sdar_vs_qwen3}, validating the efficiency of our blockwise adaptation recipe.
\paragraph{Efficiency of the Conversion Recipe.}
A critical advantage of \textsc{SDAR} is its exceptional data efficiency.
Unlike prior works like Dream~\citep{ye2025dream} that consume up to 580B tokens for adaptation, our approach achieves convergence with only \textbf{50B tokens} of continued pretraining.
This budget represents less than 1\% of the original pretraining compute, yet is sufficient to unlock the benefits of blockwise generation without degrading the backbone's capabilities.
This establishes \textsc{SDAR} as a viable pathway for equipping large-scale autoregressive models with parallel decoding capabilities.
  
这两段内容都各自更精炼一些，比如通过采用更精炼的表达，或者删掉不重要的内容

这里是对两段内容进行深度精简后的版本。通过合并句子、去除冗余词汇（如 "A critical advantage of... is"）和使用更紧凑的短语（如 "matches or outperforms" 代替 "achieves parity with or surpasses"），不仅减少了篇幅，还增强了学术表达的力度。

```latex
\paragraph{Scaling with Lossless Adaptation.}
Table~\ref{tab:comprehensive_results_superscript} shows that \textsc{SDAR} exhibits robust scaling, preserving AR backbone benefits across scales.
The conversion is largely \textit{lossless} or \textit{accretive}; at 30B, \textsc{SDAR} matches or outperforms the baseline on most benchmarks, with gains in instruction following (IFEval: +2.9) and coding (HumanEval: +2.4).
Consequently, \textsc{SDAR} significantly outperforms native diffusion baselines (e.g., LLaDA; see Appendix Table~\ref{tab:sdar_vs_qwen3}), validating our efficient adaptation recipe.

\paragraph{Efficiency of the Conversion Recipe.}
\textsc{SDAR} is exceptionally data-efficient.
Unlike Dream~\citep{ye2025dream} which requires 580B tokens, our approach converges with only \textbf{50B tokens}.
Using under 1\% of the original pretraining compute, it unlocks blockwise generation without degrading backbone capabilities.
This establishes \textsc{SDAR} as a viable pathway for equipping large-scale AR models with parallel decoding.
```

### 主要修改点：
1.  **第一段**：
    *   `achieves parity with or surpasses` $\rightarrow$ `matches or outperforms` (更简练)。
    *   `specifically, the model demonstrates improvements in...` $\rightarrow$ `with gains in...` (句式合并)。
    *   删除了 `efficiency of our blockwise` 中的 `blockwise` (上下文已明确)。

2.  **第二段**：
    *   `A critical advantage of SDAR is its...` $\rightarrow$ `SDAR is...` (直接陈述)。
    *   `Unlike prior works like Dream... that consume` $\rightarrow$ `Unlike Dream... which requires` (简化对比)。
    *   `This budget represents less than... yet is sufficient to...` $\rightarrow$ `Using under... it unlocks...` (使用分词结构更紧凑)。
\textbf{Comprehensive Performance of SDAR vs. AR.} 和 Comparing AR against SDAR across scientific and reasoning benchmarks. 我觉得有些重复啊

你是对的，标题已经说了是对比（SDAR vs. AR），紧接着正文再说“Comparing AR against SDAR”确实非常啰嗦。

建议直接**删除第二句中的“Comparing...”部分**，将“scientific and reasoning benchmarks”这一范围状语融入到原本的第三句中，这样既消除了重复，又让逻辑更连贯。

```latex
\caption{
\textbf{Comprehensive Performance of SDAR vs. AR.}
Evaluated across scientific and reasoning benchmarks, \textsc{SDAR} achieves substantial gains in structured domains (e.g., Chemistry, GPQA) while maintaining parity or improving on general tasks.
\textbf{Bold} indicates the best result.
}
```

**修改解析：**
*   删除了 `Comparing strictly AR against SDAR` 这部分重复内容。
*   将 `across scientific and reasoning benchmarks` 变成了前半句 `Evaluated across...`，直接引出结论，整段话一气呵成。
